The main claim is: If there were no correlations between subtasks, there would be no generally useful algorithms.
Common subtasks are also what make AI useful; you want AIs to pursue instrumentally convergent goals.
The main claim is: The US patent system is broadly considered to be broken, and patent-trolls make a healthy profit abusing it.

Agressively pursuing and enforcing software patents does not strike me as a sufficient or desirable means to delaying AI timelines, but I can imagine it as tool in a sliding scale of deterrence options.
